verdict.note: LIVE self-cleaning round-trip on the real Amazon Bedrock AgentCore GA Gateway control plane (bedrock-agentcore-control), driven through sentinel_harness.gateway. create_gateway(authorizer_type='AWS_IAM') -> the real GA CreateGateway op returned a gateway in CREATING; wait_gateway_ready polled the real GetGateway op until status READY; delete_gateway invoked the real DeleteGateway op (HTTP 202, DELETING); a follow-up ListGateways poll confirmed the gateway is fully gone (leftover=[]). This proves the genuine GA CreateGateway/GetGateway/DeleteGateway path end-to-end, not an offline mock. Gateway name is account+region-safe (no underscores, <=48 chars, matches the CreateGateway ([0-9a-zA-Z][-]?){1,48} rule). The probe left nothing behind and did not touch the retained demo controls (Guardrail / Cognito / CloudWatch). scenarios/scenario_named_supervisor.py was separately confirmed import-safe offline (import makes zero AWS calls; build/run/rec present). Account id scrubbed to <ACCOUNT_ID>; concrete timestamp/suffix scrubbed to <TS>/<SUFFIX>.
